result.markdown:
# Duplicates {#sec-duplicate} ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ::: It is very easy when inputting data to make mistakes, copy something in twice for example, or if someone did a lot of copy-pasting to assemble a spreadsheet (yikes!). We can check this pretty quickly :::{.callout-important collapse = "true"} ## Penguin clean names dataset ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} penguins_clean_names <- readRDS(url("https://UEABIO/5023B/raw/refs/heads/2026/files/penguin_clean_names.RDS")) ``` ::: ::: ## Duplicated rows ::: {.panel-tabset} ## dplyr ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # check for whole duplicate # rows in the data penguins_clean_names |> filter(duplicated(across(everything()))) sum() ``` ::: ``` [1] 0 ``` ## janitor ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} library(janitor) penguins_clean_names |> get_dupes() ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
|study_name | sample_number|species |region |island |stage |individual_id |clutch_completion |date_egg | culmen_length_mm| culmen_depth_mm| flipper_length_mm| body_mass_g|sex | delta_15n| delta_13c|comments | dupe_count| |:----------|-------------:|:-------|:------|:------|:-----|:-------------|:-----------------|:--------|----------------:|---------------:|-----------------:|-----------:|:---|---------:|---------:|:--------|----------:|
::: ::: ::: Great! In our dataset we have no examples of duplicated rows of data ### Working with duplications As our dataset is duplication free, lets quickly add a few dupes to our data: ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} penguins_demo <- penguins_clean_names |> slice(1:50) |> bind_rows(slice(penguins_clean_names, c(1,5,10,15,30))) ``` ::: :::{.task} ::::{.task-header} Your turn :::: ::::{.task-container} * [ ] Run the code above to make a dataset with some duplications * [ ] Re-run a duplication check :::: ::: If I did have duplications I could remove these with a few commands: :::{.panel-tabset} ## duplicated ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} # Keep only unduplicated data with ! penguins_demo |> filter(!duplicated(across(everything()))) ``` ::: ## distinct ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} penguins_demo |> distinct() ``` ::: ::: ### Counting unique entries Using the `n_distinct()` function from dplyr, you can count the number of distinct values in an R data frame using one of the following methods. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} penguins_clean_names |> summarise( n = n(), n_distinct(individual_id) ) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display}
| n| n_distinct(individual_id)| |---:|-------------------------:| | 344| 190|
::: :::
